description: Symmetric collaborative analysis workflow that orchestrates Claude and Codex into parallel independent work from an identical user-approved prompt, then merges their findings and negotiates disagreements over up to 7 rounds. Both agents receive identical input; refinement happens through the user, never via Claude-authored summaries. Use when the user invokes /duet or asks for parallel collaborative analysis with Codex.

results.query: /duet compare cursor vs offset pagination for our REST API
/duet
Run a duet on the auth refactor decision
Have Claude and Codex both analyze our caching strategy independently and reconcile their findings
Get a second independent take from Codex on this migration approach so we can reconcile
I want parallel collaborative analysis with Codex on whether to use postgres or mysql
Side-by-side analysis with Codex on which API style is better, REST or GraphQL — both of you analyze independently
Run both you and Codex on this design question in parallel and merge what you each find
Symmetric analysis with Codex: both of you weigh in independently on our auth model and we'll reconcile disagreements
Have Claude and Codex independently review the postgres-vs-mysql tradeoff and negotiate any disagreements
Write a plan for the auth migration and have Codex critique it adversarially
Have Codex review my proposed plan adversarially and we'll iterate
Ask Codex to fix this null pointer bug in the user service
Use Codex to write unit tests for this module
Implement a binary tree in Python with insertion and search methods
Analyze the parallel processing logic in worker.go for race conditions
Refactor this function for clarity and add docstrings
Compare the two implementations of the rate limiter in the file and tell me which is better
Send this README to Codex for a quick proofread pass
How do I read both files and merge them in bash, second take aside
